task_text:
Fix `TextFlow::Column` width calculation for multi-byte UTF-8 characters

In `internal/catch_textflow.cpp`, `AnsiSkippingString::preprocessString` and its iterator advance one byte at a time, counting each byte as one visible character via `m_size`. Multi-byte UTF-8 sequences (e.g., two-byte Latin Extended or three-byte CJK characters) are therefore counted as 2–4 visible characters each, causing `Column::calcLength` to calculate an inflated line width and wrap lines too early when test names or section names contain non-ASCII Unicode text. Fix the byte-counting in `preprocessString` and the `advance` method to skip UTF-8 continuation bytes (0x80–0xBF) so that `m_size` and `lineLength` reflect codepoint count rather than byte count.
solve_notes: Two-point fix in `catch_textflow.cpp`: (1) In `preprocessString`, replaced the unconditional `++m_size` with a guarded increment that only counts bytes whose top two bits are not `10` (i.e., `(byte & 0xC0) != 0x80`), which is the standard test for a UTF-8 lead byte vs. a continuation byte. (2) In `const_iterator::advance`, added a `while` loop after `++m_it` that skips continuation bytes (`(byte & 0xC0) == 0x80`), so a single call to `advance` moves past one complete codepoint rather than one raw byte. No header changes were needed. The `unadvance` method was left unchanged as the task is limited to `preprocessString` and `advance`.

search_sequence.result: Located `preprocessString` at line 33 with `++m_size` at line 53; `advance` at line 114; `calcLength` at line 147
search_sequence.reasoning: Task description named these exact symbols as the loci of the change

search_sequence.result: Loop advances one byte at a time; ANSI escape bodies are skipped but every non-escape byte unconditionally increments `m_size`; no UTF-8 awareness
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to see the exact code to know where to insert the continuation-byte guard

search_sequence.result: `m_it++` then `tryParseAnsiEscapes()` — one raw byte per call, no codepoint logic
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to see the exact code to determine where to insert the continuation-byte skip loop

key_decisions.decision: Use `(byte & 0xC0u) != 0x80u` as the guard in `preprocessString` and the inverse `== 0x80u` in `advance`
alternatives: Count only bytes >= 0xC0u (lead byte of multi-byte) or < 0x80u (ASCII)
key_decisions.reasoning: The canonical way to detect UTF-8 continuation bytes is the top-two-bit pattern 0x10xxxxxx; `(byte & 0xC0) == 0x80` captures exactly that range (0x80–0xBF). The complement `(byte & 0xC0) != 0x80` is always true for single-byte ASCII codepoints and for multi-byte lead bytes, so it correctly counts one unit per codepoint.

exploration_log.aha_moment: Recognising that both fixes follow the same bitmask idiom made it straightforward: one check skips counting in `preprocessString`, the other skips advancing past continuation bytes in `advance`.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have also checked `unadvance` for a matching fix, but the task description explicitly limits the change to `preprocessString` and `advance`.

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: AnsiSkippingString::preprocessString
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: the `++m_size` at the centre of the loop was wrapped in a UTF-8 continuation-byte guard

minimum_sufficient_defs.name: AnsiSkippingString::const_iterator::advance
minimum_sufficient_defs.reason: edited: added a while-loop to skip continuation bytes so advance moves by one codepoint

thrash_preventing_defs.name: AnsiSkippingString::const_iterator::unadvance
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: needed to verify that unadvance's sentinel-scanning logic still works correctly after the advance change; confirmed it scans back for the 0xff sentinel and does not interact with continuation bytes

thrash_preventing_defs.name: AnsiSkippingString
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirmed that `m_size` is the only visible-character counter and that `const_iterator` stores `m_it` as a `std::string::const_iterator`; knowing the field types was necessary to correctly write the `static_cast<unsigned char>` cast

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient covers the two edited methods. thrash_preventing adds `unadvance` (to confirm no backward-iteration regressions) and the `AnsiSkippingString` class declaration (to confirm the types of `m_size` and `m_it` so the cast and bitmask are written correctly). Without the class definition an agent might omit the `unsigned char` cast, causing sign-extension UB on platforms where `char` is signed.

excluded_defs.name: Column::const_iterator::calcLength
excluded_defs.reason: The task description mentions `calcLength` as a downstream consumer of the inflated width, but `calcLength` does not need to change; the fix is fully upstream in `preprocessString` and `advance`

queries.query_text: Text layout column wraps lines too early when the string contains multi-byte Unicode characters because each byte is counted as a separate visible character
queries.justification: Uses domain terms 'text layout', 'column', 'wraps lines too early', 'multi-byte Unicode', 'byte counted as visible character'. No symbol names. Pre-implementation framing of the observable bug.
The literal token `++m_size` inside `preprocessString` is the exact expression that is replaced by the fix. A developer searching for the counting logic would use this expression.
Five exact symbol names from the task description and diff. Pre-implementation: a developer tracing the width-counting logic would identify these symbols first.

queries.query_text: methods of AnsiSkippingString that modify or read m_size
queries.justification: Uses the exact class name and relationship 'methods that modify m_size'. Pre-implementation: a developer looking for where visible character count is maintained would ask for all methods touching `m_size`.
Explicit file path plus the two method names. Pre-implementation: a developer directed to `catch_textflow.cpp` would navigate there and search for the two named methods.

queries.query_text: Fix AnsiSkippingString::preprocessString and advance to count codepoints instead of bytes by skipping UTF-8 continuation bytes
queries.justification: Mixes domain concept 'codepoints instead of bytes' with exact symbols. Pre-implementation framing after the bug has been localised.

queries.query_text: AnsiSkippingString preprocessString advance m_size in catch_textflow.cpp
queries.justification: Symbol names plus the file path. Pre-implementation: a developer who has opened the file would search for these symbols together.

queries.query_text: In catch_textflow.cpp AnsiSkippingString::preprocessString increments m_size for every byte; add a guard so continuation bytes (byte & 0xC0) == 0x80 are skipped, and do the same skip in const_iterator::advance
queries.justification: Includes symbols, file path, and the concrete bitmask logic. Pre-implementation framing after full root-cause analysis.

test_selection.test_query: Find tests that exercise AnsiSkippingString::preprocessString or const_iterator::advance in catch_textflow.cpp

relevant_preexisting_tests.reason: Constructs AnsiSkippingString objects and exercises the forward iterator (++it), directly driving preprocessString (which sets m_size) and advance (called by operator++) through the changed lines
Creates Column with a known string and checks that lines wrap at the configured width; Column::calcLength depends on m_size set by preprocessString, so any miscounting would surface here
